title: 为第一代虚拟机启用密钥存储驱动器
output:
VM created: WMITEST_key_storage_drive state=0
BEFORE KsdEnabled=False
WMI ModifySecuritySettings KsdEnabled: rv=0
After pure-WMI attempt KsdEnabled=True
[GBK warning: “This feature may be removed in a future version of Hyper-V; see the Hyper-V security guide https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=875156”]
Add-VMKeyStorageDrive (cross-check): SUCCEEDED
RASD list (no rt=17 disk drive added): rt13 Virtual Keyboard / rt13 Virtual PS2 Mouse / rt24 S3 Display Controller / rt14 Synthetic Diskette Drive / rt1 (system) / rt13 Serial Controller / rt5 Emulated IDE Controller x2 / rt13 Synthetic Mouse / rt13 Synthetic Keyboard
SecuritySettingData.KsdEnabled=True
Cleanup: DestroySystem rv=4096
RESULT: PASS - KsdEnabled flipped False->True via pure WMI
notes: 密钥存储驱动器在 WMI 层并非独立的磁盘资源: 不存在 ResourceType=17 的 RASD,也没有对应的 Msvm_StorageAllocationSettingData。其状态由 Msvm_SecuritySettingData 上的布尔标志 KsdEnabled 表示。背后的隐藏 VHD 由安全服务内部托管,不暴露在 VSSD 的资源分配设置中。

启用流程: 取该虚拟机的 Msvm_SecuritySettingData,将 KsdEnabled 设为 $true,再经 Msvm_SecurityService.ModifySecuritySettings($ssd.GetText(1)) 下发。返回 rv=0 表示同步成功,KsdEnabled 由 False 变为 True,无需 Add-VMKeyStorageDrive cmdlet。
canonical MOF 将 KsdEnabled 标注为 Read/Required(只读),但 ModifySecuritySettings 实际接受并写入该字段,属于 MOF 限定符与运行时行为不一致的情形。
密钥存储驱动器是第一代(SubType:1) shielded 虚拟机的旧机制(Windows 10/Windows Server 2016 时代,与第二代虚拟机的 vTPM 相对应)。较新系统仍支持,但 Hyper-V 会输出弃用警告(fwlink 875156),提示该功能可能在未来版本移除。新部署应优先使用第二代虚拟机加 vTPM。
仅设置 KsdEnabled 标志用于启用能力; 若需完整的密钥存储驱动器,还需添加密钥存储盘(Msvm_StorageAllocationSettingData, ResourceType=17,挂接到 IDE 控制器),该盘由 Add-VMKeyStorageDrive cmdlet 一并创建。
ModifySecuritySettings 返回 4096 时需用 Wait-Job2 轮询 Job; 清理时 DestroySystem 同样可能返回 4096,需一并轮询。
title_en: Enabling the Key Storage Drive for a Generation 1 Virtual Machine
notes_en: At the WMI layer, the key storage drive is not an independent disk resource: there is no RASD with ResourceType=17, nor any corresponding Msvm_StorageAllocationSettingData. Its state is represented by the boolean flag KsdEnabled on Msvm_SecuritySettingData. The hidden VHD behind it is managed internally by the security service and is not exposed in the VSSD's resource allocation settings.

Enablement flow: obtain the VM's Msvm_SecuritySettingData, set KsdEnabled to $true, then submit it via Msvm_SecurityService.ModifySecuritySettings($ssd.GetText(1)). A return of rv=0 indicates synchronous success, KsdEnabled changes from False to True, and the Add-VMKeyStorageDrive cmdlet is not required.

The canonical MOF annotates KsdEnabled as Read/Required (read-only), but ModifySecuritySettings actually accepts and writes this field, a case where the MOF qualifier is inconsistent with the runtime behavior.

The key storage drive is the legacy mechanism for Generation 1 (SubType:1) shielded VMs (the Windows 10/Windows Server 2016 era, corresponding to the vTPM of Generation 2 VMs). It is still supported on newer systems, but Hyper-V emits a deprecation warning (fwlink 875156) noting that the feature may be removed in a future version. New deployments should prefer Generation 2 VMs with a vTPM.

Setting the KsdEnabled flag alone enables the capability; for a complete key storage drive, a key storage disk must also be added (Msvm_StorageAllocationSettingData, ResourceType=17, attached to an IDE controller), which the Add-VMKeyStorageDrive cmdlet creates together.
When ModifySecuritySettings returns 4096, the Job must be polled with Wait-Job2; during cleanup, DestroySystem may likewise return 4096 and must also be polled.
